channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: 6 street lamps faulty not working after heatwave, 19th July
item.description:
6 six lamp posts Street lamp posts numbers are 6904 6903 6907 6908 6902 6906... etc.,
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Henley Drive, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lampost damaged, 19th July
item.description:
Following road traffic incident, lampost S 516 is damaged and sparking
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 34, Selby Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Persistent fly tipping, 19th July
item.description:
For the last year this tip has been worse and worse a freezer here and there a sofa a matress it’s all the time and it always attracts rats and fox’s it’s outside of investment house on choumert road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 6, Choumert Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Gold pen tag on green telecoms cabinet, Parish Lane about 10m N of the jct with Penge Lane - by the bus shelter., 19th July
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Parish Lane, Penge, Greater London

item.title: Pile of crumpled clothes, about 3 bin bags worth, but not in bags, on the raised green billboard verge and northbound bus shelter in Parish Lane about 10m N of the jct with Paege Lane. Seen Sat 18/7/26, 19th July
item.description:
Pile of crumpled clothes, about 3 bin bags worth, but not in bags, on the raised green billboard verge and northbound bus shelter in Parish Lane about 10m N of the jct with Paege Lane. Seen Sat 18/7/26

item.title: Fire exit blocked, 19th July
item.description:
This lime bike is oarked blocking the emergency exit to My neighbiura block of flats. It is a viokation of fore kaws and after grenfeld an absokutendosgrace to see a fire escaoe on council fkats blocked and a lithum battery bike oarked so cloae to

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Livermere Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overhanging tree branches striking buses., 19th July
item.description:
The overhanging branches very loudly knock the upper deck of each bus that stops at the Heber Rd stop on Lordship Lane. The danger to passenger and pedestrian safety is obvious. It is an incident waiting to happen.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 269-281, Lordship Lane, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Kelsey Park small footbridge below the waterfall is in an absolutely disgusting state. (Photos 18/7/26), 19th July
item.description:
Kelsey Park small footbridge below the waterfall is in an absolutely disgusting state. (Photos 18/7/26) Bromley Council should be ashamed! When will you replace this footbridge? I believe that it was closed off about 5 years ago - 2021. It makes for the best viewpoint for the waterfall and fern garden. Are you just letting it become overgrown, rot to destruction, fall into the River Beck, and then pretend that there never was a bridge there? That's what it looks like! Monet did lovely paintings of a lake bridge. Bromley Council has been utterly shameful in allowing this key, waterfall viewpoint footbridge to rot. Kindly get on with replacing this bridge.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kelsey Park Avenue, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Food/item littering, 19th July
item.description:
People are littering a lot, especially their food. Bones, moldy bread anything toxic - this calls in for pest and tryna feed foxes. Peoples dogs eats these food especially moldy food and they get extremely sick. It’s toxic makes our dogs sick, brings pest
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Gopsall Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Previous bus stop now removed, 19th July
item.description:
The bus stop going towards Belsize Park which used to be outside the Roundhouse in Chalk Farm Road has been discontinued. This is no good for people with mobility problems or the elderly. It may facilitate people on bicycles who do not have these problems. Please reinstate this bus stop as soon as possible for the people who are vulnerable and need it, who may not have the loudest voices.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 86, Chalk Farm Road, Chalk Farm, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 7 abandoned Forest hire bikes upright and lying down in the Bromley Council flowering trees landscaped pedestrian section of Hardings Lane, Penge at the junction of Lennard Rd., 19th July
item.description:
7 abandoned Forest hire bikes upright and lying down in the Bromley Council flowering trees landscaped pedestrian section of Hardings Lane, Penge at the junction of Lennard Rd. Seen 11.20am Saturday 18/7/26 Please remove these pedestrian hazards and eyesores in the specially landscaped area. Do not permit bikes to be dumped here by your contractors or hirers again! What an eyesore dumping ground!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hardings Lane, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Damaged and hazardous Council pedestrian guard railing fronting Shortlands Station., 18th July
item.description:
Seemingly impact-damaged Visirail pedestrian guard railing panel in Shortlands Rd at the traffic signal jct with Bromley Rd fronting Shortlands Station. Two of the rails are now spike hazards into where pedestrians walk at this busy location. Please attend to this as a matter of urgency. Seen 10.45am Saturday 18/7/26

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Shortlands Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Piles of plant waste now housing rats. On the outside of car park behind shops, near bins and drying area, 18th July
item.description:
Piles of plant waste now housing rats. Been here at least 6 months

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Masons Road, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping and damaged fencing, 18th July
item.description:
Fjere is a small amount of fly tipping and also damage to the fencing along the alleyway.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wellan Close, Blackfen, Greater London

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Burrell Row, Beckenham, Greater London

item.title: Flytipped toilet and liquid container at parking spaces on Kempe road and masons road, 18th July
item.description:
Flytipped toilet and liquid container (been here months) at parking spaces on Kempe road and masons road.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Masons Road, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing bin, 18th July
item.description:
The bin just outside the gate into Danson Park, opposite Danson Crescent, is overflowing and some rubbish has been left next to it.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Danson Lane, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Burst pipe middle of main road Chelsea Embankment, 18th July
item.description:
There is water coming out from the middle of the main road right in front of Chelsea Quay. It has created flooding which is growing. I noticed it on Friday and today Saturday the flood had extended. I have also reported on Thames Water website (have not heard back) but putting it in here just in case.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chelsea Embankment, Chelsea, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lights broken - not safe, 18th July
item.description:
Weeks after reporting these footpath lights not working they are still not working causing danger to potential trip and full hazards and also personal safety as it is pitch black.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Magpie Hall Lane, Bromley Common, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
